audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot tabernacle instruction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for curtains, coverings, frames, bars, veil, screen, and spatial arrangement of holy/most holy.

verses.restored: And dwelling-place you shall make: ten curtains of fine twisted linen, blue, purple, and scarlet, with cherubim, work of skilled designer, you shall make them.
Length of one curtain twenty-eight cubits, width four cubits; one measure for all curtains.
Five curtains shall be joined one to another, and five curtains joined one to another.
You shall make loops of blue on edge of one curtain at end of set, and likewise on edge of outer curtain in second set.
Fifty loops you shall make in one curtain, and fifty loops at edge of curtain in second set, loops opposite each other.
Make fifty gold clasps and join curtains one to another with clasps, so dwelling-place shall be one.
You shall make curtains of goats' hair for tent over dwelling-place; eleven curtains you shall make.
Length of one curtain thirty cubits, width four cubits; one measure for eleven curtains.
Join five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves, and fold sixth curtain at front of tent.
Make fifty loops on edge of one outer curtain of set, and fifty loops on edge of curtain of second set.
Make fifty bronze clasps, bring clasps into loops, and join tent so it shall be one.
Surplus hanging over curtains of tent, half curtain surplus, shall hang over back of dwelling-place.
Cubit on one side and cubit on other side of surplus length of curtains of tent shall hang over sides of dwelling-place, to cover it.
Make covering for tent of reddened ram skins, and covering of durable hides above.
You shall make frames for dwelling-place of acacia wood, standing upright.
Ten cubits length of frame, and one and a half cubits width of one frame.
Two tenons for one frame, joined each to other; so you shall do for all frames of dwelling-place.
Make frames for dwelling-place: twenty frames for south side, toward south.
Forty silver bases you shall make under twenty frames, two bases under one frame for its two tenons and two bases under another frame for its two tenons.
For second side of dwelling-place, north side, twenty frames,
and their forty silver bases, two bases under one frame and two bases under another frame.
For rear of dwelling-place westward, make six frames.
Make two frames for corners of dwelling-place in rear.
They shall be doubled below and together complete at top into one ring; so it shall be for them both, for two corners.
There shall be eight frames and their silver bases, sixteen bases, two bases under one frame and two bases under another frame.
Make bars of acacia wood: five for frames of one side of dwelling-place,
five bars for frames of second side of dwelling-place, and five bars for frames of side of dwelling-place at rear westward.
Middle bar in midst of frames shall pass through from end to end.
Overlay frames with gold, make their rings of gold as housings for bars, and overlay bars with gold.
Set up dwelling-place according to its judgment/pattern that you were shown on mountain.
Make veil of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen; work of skilled designer; it shall be made with cherubim.
Put it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold, their hooks gold, on four silver bases.
Put veil under clasps, and bring ark of testimony there inside veil; veil shall separate for you between Holy Place and Holy of Holies.
Put cover upon ark of testimony in Holy of Holies.
Set table outside veil, and lampstand opposite table on south side of dwelling-place; table put on north side.
Make screen for entrance of tent, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, work of embroiderer.
Make for screen five pillars of acacia, overlay them with gold, their hooks gold, and cast for them five bronze bases.

translation_notes: Dwelling-place/mishkan is a layered portable sacred space, not monumental empire architecture.
Repeated 'so it shall be one' emphasizes joined unity through many pieces.
Cherubim on curtains and veil mark guarded holy presence, echoing Eden/ark imagery.
Veil separates Holy Place and Holy of Holies; spatial holiness should be preserved without overreading later theology too early.
safety_note: This chapter is technical sacred craft. Avoid flattening it into mere decoration; it is ordered, portable holiness for a liberated wilderness people.
